hero.title: Haze Piece Wiki
hero.description: Explore the Haze Piece and Haze Seas Roblox rework with active codes, Devil Fruits, races, Haki, weapons, bosses, maps, and beginner progression guides.

gameFeature.title: Haze Piece Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Sail the Haze Seas, roll Devil Fruits, master Haki, race rerolls, and farm bosses across islands in this Roblox pirate action RPG rework of Haze Piece.

hazePieceCodes.title: Haze Piece Codes for Haze Seas
hazePieceCodes.subtitle: Redeem the current Haze Seas code set for Race Spins, Gems, Cash, and EXP boosts. Open the in-game Menu, tap the Treasure Chest or Gift icon, paste the code, and press Redeem.
hazePieceCodes.intro: Haze Piece now runs on Roblox as Haze Seas, so players search both names when hunting codes. Redeem active update codes before leveling so EXP boosts, Race Spins, Gems, and Cash support early progression.
hazePieceCodes.howToRedeemTitle: How to Redeem Haze Piece Codes
hazePieceCodes.howToRedeem: Open the in-game Menu, select the Treasure Chest or Gift icon, paste a working Haze Seas code, and press Redeem. Codes are case-sensitive, so copy them exactly as shown.

hazePieceTrelloDiscord.title: Haze Piece Trello, Discord, Roblox, X, and YouTube
hazePieceTrelloDiscord.subtitle: Use the official Haze Seas link hub for update news, code drops, Trello data, Roblox group rewards, and video showcases.
hazePieceTrelloDiscord.intro: These are the core official destinations a Haze Piece player needs: the Roblox game page, the official Trello, the Discord invite, the Haze Studios Roblox community, the RealHazeStudios X account, and the Haze Studios YouTube channel.

items.label: Official Trello
items.bestFor: Fruits, weapons, fighting styles, races, NPCs, bosses, items, and system references

items.label: Discord Server
items.bestFor: Codes, sneak peeks, update posts, community help, bug reports, and party finding

items.label: Roblox Game Page
items.bestFor: Play button, game description, platform support, servers, passes, and live title changes

items.label: Haze Studios Roblox Community
items.bestFor: Group membership, community notices, group-linked rewards, and developer posts

items.label: RealHazeStudios on X
items.bestFor: Short announcements, update reminders, release posts, and development media

items.label: Haze Studios YouTube
items.bestFor: Trailers, fruit showcases, cutscenes, release videos, and visual update previews

hazePieceBeginnerGuide.title: Haze Piece Beginner Guide for Haze Seas
hazePieceBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Follow a simple early-game route: redeem codes, complete island quests, choose one damage path, get a usable Fruit, and unlock the core mobility and Haki tools.
hazePieceBeginnerGuide.intro: Haze Seas rewards focused progression. New Haze Piece players should follow island quests, invest in one primary damage path, keep Defense as a secondary stat, and unlock mobility or Haki tools as soon as their level requirements are met.

items.title: Start from the Roblox game page
items.summary: Launch Haze Seas from the official Roblox experience and choose your early direction as a Pirate or Marine.
checklist: Like and favorite the game before claiming starter rewards.
Join the official Haze Studios Roblox community for group-linked rewards.
Use PC, mobile, or Xbox controls; the Roblox page lists mobile and Xbox support.

items.title: Redeem codes before grinding
items.summary: Open the Menu, select the Treasure Chest or Gift icon, enter a working code, and press Redeem.
checklist: Redeem Race Spin codes before locking in a long session.
Use EXP boost codes before quest loops or boss farming.
Use Cash and Gem codes before rolling Fruits or buying early upgrades.

items.title: Farm quests on the current island
items.summary: Quest NPCs are the fastest beginner route because they reward EXP and Cash while pushing you toward the next level gate.
checklist: Accept the island quest instead of farming random enemies.
Clear the required enemies or boss, then turn in the quest.
Move to the next highlighted destination when the game opens a new area.

items.title: Build around one main damage stat
items.summary: Choose Fruit, Sword, or Combat as your main damage lane and keep Defense as the secondary stat.
checklist: Fruit build: prioritize Fruit, then Defense.
Sword build: prioritize Sword, then Defense.
Fighting Style build: prioritize Combat, then Defense.

items.title: Get a usable Devil Fruit early
items.summary: Use Cynthia the Fruit Dealer, starter rewards, tree spawns, daily rewards, or trading to get a Fruit that speeds up quest clearing.
checklist: Fruit Dealer Cynthia can roll a random Fruit for Cash or Gems.
World Fruits spawn under trees every 60 minutes and despawn after 20 minutes.
VIP servers can hold up to 5 Fruit spawns at once.

items.title: Unlock key movement and Haki tools
items.summary: Mobility and Haki make mid-game travel and Logia fights smoother, so do not ignore trainers while chasing bigger damage numbers.
checklist: Flash Step comes from the trainer on Clown Island at Level 40.
Buso Haki comes from Old Man Silver in Luke Town at Level 350.
Observation Haki comes from the trainer on Marine Base Town Island at Level 600.

items.title: Use bosses after your quest loop is stable
items.summary: Bosses are worth farming once you can clear them safely because they add EXP, Cash, and drop chances for swords or accessories.
checklist: Fight bosses you naturally meet while leveling.
Do not abandon efficient quests just to chase rare drops too early.
Upgrade your main damage path before pushing into harder islands.

hazePieceFruitsTierList.title: Haze Piece Fruits Tier List
hazePieceFruitsTierList.subtitle: Compare the current Haze Seas Fruit roster by grinding speed, boss value, PvP pressure, mobility, and overall progression value.
hazePieceFruitsTierList.intro: Haze Seas lists 35 Fruits in the Roblox experience description. Fruits spawn every 60 minutes, despawn after 20 minutes, and VIP servers can hold up to 5 Fruit spawns, so this tier grid focuses on what Haze Piece players should keep, trade, or replace during progression.
hazePieceFruitsTierList.tierNote: This Haze Piece fruit tier list reflects our recommended ranking for common progression scenarios. It is a community-style guide, not an official Haze Seas classification.

tiers.label: Meta Picks
tiers.summary: Best all-around Fruits for high damage, farming, PvP pressure, mobility, or late-game scaling.

fruits.name: Dragon
fruits.note: Heavy AoE and transformation value make it a premium endgame Fruit.

fruits.name: Dough
fruits.note: Wide-area attacks make it one of the cleanest Fruits for clearing enemy groups.

fruits.name: Gum
fruits.note: Gear-style progression gives it strong long-term combat value.

fruits.name: Electricity
fruits.note: Quick lightning moves and repositioning tools make it strong for active combat.

fruits.name: Phoenix
fruits.note: Flight, mobility, and strong combat tools keep it useful across multiple modes.

fruits.name: Shadow
fruits.note: Area pressure and repositioning make it excellent for grinding and fights.

fruits.name: Okuchi
fruits.note: A new high-value Fruit tied to the Okuchi update and current code cycle.

tiers.label: Very Strong
tiers.summary: Reliable Fruits that can carry farming, bossing, or PvP without requiring the absolute rarest pull.

fruits.name: Darkness
fruits.note: Strong area control makes grouped enemy farming efficient.

fruits.name: Magma
fruits.note: High damage and area pressure make it a strong farm-and-boss Fruit.

fruits.name: Venom
fruits.note: Good pressure over time and strong damage uptime.

fruits.name: Light
fruits.note: Fast movement and solid damage make it one of the easiest beginner upgrades.

fruits.name: Leopard
fruits.note: A strong combat transformation option for aggressive players.

fruits.name: Tremor
fruits.note: Large-area attacks help with clustered enemies and steady PvE damage.

fruits.name: Wolf
fruits.note: A strong newer roster Fruit for players who like direct combat pressure.

tiers.label: Good Progression
tiers.summary: Useful Fruits for early-to-mid progression, farming comfort, or specific playstyles.

fruits.name: Soul
fruits.note: Useful for area damage and flexible combat routing.

fruits.name: Magnet
fruits.note: Good damage and control, though slower than top-tier Fruits.

fruits.name: Ice
fruits.note: Reliable control tools make it safe for questing.

fruits.name: Flame
fruits.note: Easy early damage and area attacks help new players level faster.

fruits.name: Gas
fruits.note: Good PvE output with a less flexible PvP feel.

fruits.name: Gravity
fruits.note: Useful damage, but enemy displacement can slow farming routes.

fruits.name: Buddha
fruits.note: A sturdy early option while searching for stronger Fruits.

fruits.name: Snow
fruits.note: Comfortable enough for progression before higher-tier pulls.

fruits.name: Mammoth
fruits.note: Works for steady clears but lacks top-tier speed.

fruits.name: String
fruits.note: Decent mobility and utility, with lower farming speed than stronger options.

fruits.name: Love
fruits.note: Playable for utility-focused users but not the fastest progression pick.

tiers.label: Replace Later
tiers.summary: Early-game or situational Fruits that work, but should be replaced once stronger options appear.

fruits.name: Operation
fruits.note: Decent tools, but weaker overall pressure than higher-tier Fruits.

fruits.name: Paw
fruits.note: Usable damage, but lower area coverage for fast farming.

fruits.name: Smoke
fruits.note: A workable starter Fruit while upgrading into stronger Logia-style picks.

fruits.name: Sand
fruits.note: A simple early option that loses value as stronger Fruits unlock.

fruits.name: Barrier
fruits.note: Defensive value is useful, but damage output is limited.

fruits.name: Bomb
fruits.note: Basic burst damage before better farming Fruits are available.

fruits.name: Chop
fruits.note: Good only as a temporary early Fruit.

tiers.label: Weak Starters
tiers.summary: Low-output Fruits that should be replaced quickly for smoother questing and boss progression.

fruits.name: Clear
fruits.note: Limited direct damage makes it weak for progression.

fruits.name: Spin
fruits.note: Movement value does not compensate for low damage.

fruits.name: Spike
fruits.note: Easy to use but quickly outclassed by almost every stronger Fruit.

hazePieceFruitsAndStockGuide.title: Haze Piece Fruits, Spawn Timers, and Stock
hazePieceFruitsAndStockGuide.subtitle: Track every Haze Seas fruit, the world spawn timer, the despawn window, and the main ways to get fruits.
hazePieceFruitsAndStockGuide.intro: Fruits spawn under trees every 60 minutes and despawn after 20 minutes. VIP servers can hold up to five spawned fruits, and players can also chase specific powers through the fruit dealer gacha, trading, and starter reward routes.

rules.name: World Fruit Spawn
rules.details: Fruits spawn under trees across islands.
rules.note: Start a timer after server entry or a confirmed pickup to plan the next fruit check.

rules.name: Fruit Despawn
rules.details: Unclaimed fruits disappear after the despawn window.
rules.note: Run your tree routes quickly once a spawn window opens.

rules.name: VIP Server Fruit Limit
rules.details: VIP servers can hold up to five spawned fruits at once.
rules.note: VIP fruit farming is most efficient with a simple repeating island route.

routes.name: Cynthia Fruit Dealer Gacha
routes.details: Roll fruits at Cynthia's dealer spots, including the Starter Island dealer and later island dealers.
routes.note: Best for players who want active rolls instead of waiting on world spawns.

routes.name: Trading
routes.details: Trade with other players to target fruits you do not want to roll or wait for.
routes.note: Best for rare fruit targeting once you understand current demand.

fruits.name: Okuchi
fruits.note: Available across world spawns, dealer gacha, and trading routes.

fruits.name: Wolf
fruits.note: Worth tracking alongside transformation-focused fruits.

fruits.name: Clear
fruits.note: Common early fruit route target.

fruits.name: Chop
fruits.note: Common early fruit route target.

fruits.name: Spin
fruits.note: Starter-friendly fruit to replace once stronger options appear.

fruits.name: Spike
fruits.note: Starter-friendly fruit to replace once stronger options appear.

fruits.name: Bomb
fruits.note: Early combat fruit for basic progression.

fruits.name: Barrier
fruits.note: Defensive-style fruit route.

fruits.name: Paw
fruits.note: Utility and combat fruit route.

fruits.name: Smoke
fruits.note: Early elemental-style farming option.

fruits.name: Sand
fruits.note: Early elemental-style farming option.

fruits.name: String
fruits.note: Mobility and combo-style fruit route.

fruits.name: Mammoth
fruits.note: Transformation-style fruit route.

fruits.name: Buddha
fruits.note: Large-form combat fruit route.

fruits.name: Snow
fruits.note: Elemental-style fruit route.

fruits.name: Tremor
fruits.note: AoE-heavy fruit route for farming and boss pressure.

fruits.name: Gas
fruits.note: Area-control fruit route.

fruits.name: Gravity
fruits.note: Gravity-control fruit route.

fruits.name: Flame
fruits.note: Strong early-to-mid elemental farming option.

fruits.name: Shadow
fruits.note: Strong farming, mobility, and PvP route.

fruits.name: Light
fruits.note: Useful for travel, mobility, and quick farming.

fruits.name: Operation
fruits.note: Utility-heavy fruit route.

fruits.name: Ice
fruits.note: Control and farming fruit route.

fruits.name: Electricity
fruits.note: High-value AoE, mobility, and farming route.

fruits.name: Magma
fruits.note: High-damage elemental farming route.

fruits.name: Love
fruits.note: Utility and ranged-pressure fruit route.

fruits.name: Gum
fruits.note: Core route for Gear progression.

fruits.name: Darkness
fruits.note: Strong crowd-control and farming route.

fruits.name: Magnet
fruits.note: Damage-focused fruit route.

fruits.name: Phoenix
fruits.note: Mobility, healing-style, and PvP pressure route.

fruits.name: Soul
fruits.note: AoE and utility fruit route.

fruits.name: Leopard
fruits.note: Transformation-style route for active combat.

fruits.name: Venom
fruits.note: Damage-over-time and boss-pressure route.

fruits.name: Dragon
fruits.note: High-value transformation and late-game chase fruit.

fruits.name: Dough
fruits.note: High-value farming and boss route.

hazePieceRacesAndHakiGuide.title: Haze Piece Races, Race Rerolls, and Haki Unlocks
hazePieceRacesAndHakiGuide.subtitle: Compare D. Clan, Dragonborne, Demon, Human, Beastborne, Skyborne, Fishborne, and the main Haki progression paths.
hazePieceRacesAndHakiGuide.intro: Races shape passive stats, mobility, survivability, and access to Conqueror's Haki. Buso Haki boosts weapon and fighting-style damage, Observation Haki adds dodges, and D. Clan unlocks Conqueror's Haki for players chasing the rarest race route.

haki.name: Buso Haki
haki.unlock: Old Man Silver / Buso Trainer at Logue City
haki.baseEffect: +2.5% weapon and fighting-style damage while active
haki.upgradePath: Earn EXP with Buso Haki, then complete the Level 5 quest at Flower Capital by taking 1,000 hits and paying 200 Gems.
haki.maxEffect: Level 5 grants +15% damage and +10% damage resistance.
haki.bestFor: Sword builds, fighting-style builds, Elemental-user counterplay, and boss farming

haki.name: Observation Haki
haki.unlock: Observation Haki Trainer at Marine Base Town
haki.upgradePath: Dodge attacks to gain EXP, then complete the Level 6 quest at Flower Capital by defeating 5 players, 10 Island Bosses, and paying $10M.
haki.maxEffect: Level 6 reaches 8 dodges and adds long-range player information.
haki.bestFor: PvP survival, boss safety, and late-game scouting

haki.name: Conqueror's Haki
haki.baseEffect: Deals damage to nearby weaker enemies and stuns them briefly.
haki.upgradePath: D. Clan V2 reduces the stamina cost from 250 to 200.
haki.maxEffect: No separate level track; strength comes from D. Clan access and V2 stamina efficiency.
haki.bestFor: PvP pressure, crowd control against weaker enemies, and rare-race builds

races.name: D. Clan
races.v1: +5% Health, +5% Stamina, Conqueror's Haki (250 Stamina cost)
races.v2: +10% Health, +10% Stamina, Conqueror's Haki (200 Stamina cost)
races.rerollSources: Daily rewards, drops, codes, and AFK Chamber race reroll routes
races.bestFor: Best all-around race for survivability, stamina, and exclusive Conqueror's Haki access

races.name: Dragonborne
races.v1: +10% Health, +10% Health Regen, +25% first Skywalk boost
races.rerollSources: Daily rewards, drops, codes, and AFK Chamber race reroll routes
races.bestFor: Tankier builds, travel comfort, and a strong non-D. Clan route

races.name: Demon
races.v2: +3% Lifesteal and 10% chance to summon a Hellfire Circle on dash
races.rerollSources: Daily rewards, drops, codes, and AFK Chamber race reroll routes
races.bestFor: Long fights, sustain builds, and self-healing in PvE or PvP

races.name: Human
races.v2: +50% Walkspeed while under 70% HP and +1 Observation Dodge
races.rerollSources: Daily rewards, drops, codes, and AFK Chamber race reroll routes
races.bestFor: Budget mobility, emergency escapes, and Observation Haki support

races.name: Beastborne
races.rerollSources: Daily rewards, drops, codes, and AFK Chamber race reroll routes
races.bestFor: Fast movement, dash-heavy play, and stamina comfort

races.name: Skyborne
races.v2: Glide ability and doubled first Skywalk power
races.rerollSources: Daily rewards, drops, codes, and AFK Chamber race reroll routes
races.bestFor: Island travel, aerial movement, and exploration

races.name: Fishborne
races.v2: 2x Swim Speed, 2x Walkspeed for 4 seconds after leaving water, and water damage immunity for fruit users
races.rerollSources: Daily rewards, drops, codes, and AFK Chamber race reroll routes
races.bestFor: Sea travel, fruit-user safety, and water-heavy routing

raceV2Route.name: Race V2 Unlock Route
raceV2Route.unlock: Race Engineer on the first Sea 2 island
raceV2Route.requirements: Level 2200+, defeat a Sea Beast, collect 50 chests, defeat the Zenith Boss, then pay $1M and 100 Gems
raceV2Route.baseEffect: Unlocks each race's V2 passive route.
raceV2Route.maxEffect: V2 upgrades turn race passives into core progression tools.

hazePieceSwordsAndFightingStyles.title: Haze Piece Swords, Weapons, and Fighting Styles
hazePieceSwordsAndFightingStyles.subtitle: Plan a sword route, compare fighting styles, and avoid wasting upgrades on gear that does not match your build.
hazePieceSwordsAndFightingStyles.intro: Swords, fruits, and fighting styles are the three main damage lanes in Haze Seas. Pick one main weapon lane, unlock its mastery moves, then pair it with a fruit or style that covers the range your build lacks.

tiers.label: S+ Swords
tiers.summary: Endgame swords and final-form upgrades that define a sword-main's late game.

entries.name: Dual Dark Blade
entries.howToGet: Craft at Doran the Forge using 20 Ancient Blade Relics, 25M Bounty, 300 Mastery on Dark Blade V2, 1 Abyssal Eye, and 20M Beli.
entries.bestFor: Late-game PvE, boss damage, and PvP burst

entries.name: Three Sword Style V2
entries.howToGet: Craft with 3SS V1, 300 Mastery, and 5 Ancient Blade Relics. 3SS V1 needs Zenith, Shusui, and Enma at 300 Mastery before the Rock Shrine at Land of Gods.
entries.bestFor: Sword-main progression, burst damage, and endgame farming

entries.name: Gryphon
entries.howToGet: Drops from Red Emperor with a 5% drop chance.
entries.bestFor: PvP pressure, combo routes, and flexible PvE

entries.name: Ace
entries.howToGet: Drops from the Final Quest reward pool with a 0.1% regular chance or 1% Infinity chance.
entries.bestFor: Rare weapon collectors and high-burst sword builds

tiers.label: S Swords
tiers.summary: Premium all-round swords that carry sword builds through mid and late progression.

entries.name: Dark Blade V2
entries.howToGet: Sacrifice a Legendary or higher fruit to Nikk0lapz in Foggy Castle, defeat 3SS Boss or Enma, pay 50 Gems to summon the Dark Blade Boss, then defeat the boss.
entries.bestFor: All-round PvE, PvP, and mid-to-late progression

entries.name: Zenith
entries.howToGet: Guaranteed drop from the Zenith Boss during the Shadow Island storm event.
entries.bestFor: Sea 2 sword progression and 3SS V1 preparation

entries.name: Golden Staff V2
entries.howToGet: Upgrade Golden Staff V1 with 300 Mastery, 1 Raijin Core, and 2M Beli.
entries.bestFor: Electricity fruit synergy and ranged-melee pressure

tiers.label: A Swords
tiers.summary: Solid route swords that unlock the Three Sword Style and Sea 2 progression chains.

entries.name: Enma
entries.howToGet: 10% drop chance from the Enma Boss.
entries.bestFor: Required route toward Three Sword Style

entries.name: Shusui
entries.howToGet: 5% drop chance from Ryummy at Thriller Boat.
entries.bestFor: Required route toward Three Sword Style

entries.name: Dough Trident
entries.howToGet: Drops from the Dough Boss in Mirror World.
entries.bestFor: Dough Island progression and boss loot farming

tiers.label: A Fighting Styles
tiers.summary: Strong fighting styles with V2 routes, covering AoE, stun, and aggressive melee.

entries.name: Black Leg
entries.howToGet: Buy from the Black Leg trainer for 50,000 Beli.
entries.v2Route: Evolve at Sage of Combat with 300 Mastery, 2 Phantom Lanterns, and 5M Beli.
entries.bestFor: General farming, AoE combos, and melee builds

entries.name: Electro
entries.v2Route: Evolve with 300 Mastery, 2 Raijin's Cores, and 500 Gems.
entries.bestFor: Stun-lock combos, sword pairing, and active PvP

entries.name: Dragon Claw
entries.howToGet: Buy from the Dragon Master for 150 Gems.
entries.v2Route: Evolve at Sage of Combat with 300 Mastery plus the tied material and funds.
entries.bestFor: Burst melee, aggressive PvP, and premium style routing

tiers.label: B Fighting Styles
tiers.summary: Reliable budget styles for heavy hits and hybrid melee-ranged pressure.

entries.name: Fishman
entries.v2Route: Evolve with 300 Mastery, 2 Seabeast Hearts, and 5M Beli.
entries.bestFor: Heavy hits, water-themed builds, and close-range pressure

entries.name: Cyborg
entries.howToGet: Buy from the Cyborg Scientist for 800,000 Beli.
entries.v2Route: Evolve with 300 Mastery, 2 Saturn's Rings, and 5M Beli.

hazePieceBossDropsAndMap.title: Haze Piece Boss Drops, Islands, and Progression Map
hazePieceBossDropsAndMap.subtitle: Track island bosses, super bosses, summon items, rare accessories, weapons, and Second Sea progression blockers.
hazePieceBossDropsAndMap.intro: Boss farming is the practical route for accessories, weapons, summon items, materials, gems, bounty, and progression unlocks. Start with fast 30-second island bosses, then move into super bosses once your build can deal real damage.

groups.heading: Sea 1 Island Bosses
groups.summary: Fast-respawning island bosses that fund your early accessory and weapon routes.

bosses.note: First accessory farming route.
Early accessory route after Starter Island.
Important early sword farming target.
Accessory route for early durability.
Useful island for both boss farming and Haki routing.
Accessory target before stronger super boss routes.
Observation Haki island route.
Accessory farming stop before Marine HQ.
Farm Tremor Key here to unlock the Tremor Girl super boss route.
Key weapon route for players building toward Golden Staff upgrades.
Farm Match here to start the Fire Fist summon route.
Accessory farming route in the mid Sea 1 chain.
Material farming stop for later crafting routes.
Farm Fork here for Fishman Trident routing.
Scroll route for Raiu weapon progression.
Gear route checkpoint for Gum users.
Important Shusui farming route for Three Sword Style.

groups.heading: Sea 1 Super Bosses
groups.summary: Summon-chain and timed super bosses that gate premium weapons and accessories.

bosses.note: Book material route for Dual Sword Style V2.

bosses.spawn: Summon by dropping Lava Ore in the cave cauldron
bosses.note: Mace route and early super boss checkpoint.

bosses.spawn: Use Tremor Key at the locked Whitebeard Pirates gate
drops: Bisento V2 when owning Bisento V1
bosses.note: Bisento upgrade and Tremor route checkpoint.

bosses.spawn: Use Match from Warden Boss, light the Tall Woods campfire, then place four pearls on the island totems
bosses.note: Summon-chain boss route with fruit and accessory rewards.

bosses.spawn: Respawns in 30 to 120 minutes in Sea 1
drops: Sea Beast Hammer V1 - 50% drop chance
bosses.note: Major material and weapon farming target.

groups.heading: Sea 2 Island Bosses
groups.summary: Sea 2 island bosses that drop orbs and fragments for super boss summons.

bosses.note: Important stop for Three Sword Style and Dark Blade routes.
Farm Dragon Orb here to summon Dragon Boss.
Mirror Fragment route for Dough Boss access.
Material route for Snow-related progression.
Accessory farming target in the Sea 2 chain.

groups.heading: Sea 2 Super Bosses
groups.summary: Endgame super bosses carrying the strongest swords, weapons, and titles.

bosses.note: Required sword route for Three Sword Style progression.

bosses.spawn: Place Dragon Orb on the Dragon Island altar
drops: Drums of Liberation - 100% drop chance when using Gum Fruit
bosses.note: Major Gear progression boss for Gum users.

bosses.spawn: Use Mirror Fragment route, complete Syn NPC doughnut quest, open Mirror World, then spawn through Spex NPC using 50 Gems
bosses.note: High-value Dough route with weapon and accessory drops.

bosses.spawn: Complete the Dark Blade Upgrader route at Foggy Castle
bosses.note: Dark Blade upgrade route for premium sword users.

bosses.spawn: Spawns automatically during a storm on Shadow Island
bosses.note: Best guaranteed sword target for Three Sword Style preparation.

bosses.spawn: Respawns in 20 to 60 minutes in Sea 2
drops: Sea Beast Heart material - 25% drop chance
Sea Beast Hammer V2 - 3.5% drop chance
bosses.note: Important material route for Fishman V2 and late weapon farming.

faq.title: Haze Piece
faq.subtitle: The top questions Haze Piece and Haze Seas players ask before sailing out.

questions.question: What is Haze Piece and how is it related to Haze Seas?
questions.answer: Haze Piece is the original Roblox pirate action RPG, and Haze Seas is its official rework by Haze Studios. Haze Piece Wiki covers both names so players can find codes, fruits, and guides no matter which title they search.

questions.question: Where do I redeem Haze Piece codes in Haze Seas?
questions.answer: In Haze Seas, open the in-game Menu, select the Treasure Chest or Gift icon, paste a Haze Piece code, and press Redeem. Codes are case-sensitive, so copy them exactly as written on the Haze Piece Wiki codes list.

questions.question: How many Devil Fruits are in Haze Piece?
questions.answer: Haze Piece and the Haze Seas rework list 35 Devil Fruits. Fruits spawn under trees every 60 minutes, despawn after 20 minutes, and VIP servers can hold up to 5 Fruit spawns at once.

questions.question: Can I play Haze Piece on mobile and Xbox?
questions.answer: Yes. Haze Seas, the Haze Piece rework, is a Roblox experience and supports PC, mobile, and Xbox controls, so the same Haze Piece progression route works across platforms.

questions.question: What are Race Spins in Haze Piece?
questions.answer: Race Spins let Haze Piece players reroll their race, with rare options like D. Clan offering extra value. Several active Haze Seas codes grant Race Spins, so redeem them before you commit to a long session.

questions.question: How do I unlock Haki in Haze Piece?
questions.answer: Haze Piece Haki unlocks by level: Flash Step at Level 40 on Clown Island, Buso Haki from Old Man Silver in Luke Town at Level 350, and Observation Haki on Marine Base Town Island at Level 600.
